item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda liked the idea here, but the grammar and general outline is way to choppy for me. The interview reads more like bullet points than how actual people talk, and the mtf log seems more like the way d-class would act. -1 for now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed a few places where you could split sentences or adjust the style, but overall it seems good.

originally located on the East coast of Madagascar

This part confused me, though. Is it no longer in Madagascar?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a minor edit, just fix these yourself when you see them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The SCP-XXXX is still there in the last collapsible.

Besides that, I liked the work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have to agree with the above. The idea is really good, but there are enough tonal issues that it makes it hard for this to really suck me in.

- Detailed Critique
- A bit long

To start with, I'm not the most experienced, so take all of what I say with a grain of salt. Given that, here are my in-depth thoughts:

As mentioned, the repetition of 'SCP-3227's toxin' and in general the word 'toxic'. Just one example, you specify the tongue contains 'alkaloid toxins', which is redundant as you've already clarified that it's alkaloid based. A possible solution is to declare the toxins as 'SCCP-3227-1' or such, as it seems obviously slightly anomalous. Then you can describe it once, and just refer to the designation elsewhere.

The tonal issues are a bit harder for me to pin down. I'll pull a couple lines that stand out and specify my thoughts on those lines in specific

"by a group of independent researchers who wandered into the jungle in search of new species of fauna." - The phrasing of 'wandered into the jungle' implies they just kind of… walked in one day, as a group. If they're researchers, they're probably not just deciding one day to walk randomly around a jungle, they're planning to on a specific day walk randomly around the jungle.

"Following this incident, humans were forbidden to interact with SCP-3227 in any destructive manner" - would probably be better as 'humans are', as the impression I get is that the ban is still in place

"While containment is a top priority for SCP-3227, the Foundation has also made it a top priority to utilize SCP-3227 to its maximum potential." - I don't think you can have two 'top' priorities. Maybe a top priority and a secondary priority, or 'While containment is top priority, the foundation is willing to utilize SCP-3227 as long as it doesn't interfere with that priority.'

The information in the article could probably do with some re-organization, ditto what Sindar said.

Now, the things I liked about this: The agent interview dialogue is pretty good, especially the last couple lines I liked. The containment protocol I have no qualms with, they look good. And as noted, I really like the overall idea of this. It's a combination of weird and fascinating, and as noted the possibility of it spreading lends an element of continuous threat, no just leaving it alone.

In conclusion I'm going to Novote, but I have hope this could be quite good with a bit of fixing up.

(Also, there's an SCP-XXXX left at the end of the article.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the wording needs some work. A bit too much "SCP-3227's toxin" so it just sounded a little repetitive. I love the concept though, and the idea that it could spread after a crash during sample transport makes the havoc it could cause exciting. It'll get a +1 from me, but I really hope to see this SCP get some love after getting a little bit more work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tentative upvote from me. I like the anomaly itself very much, but the article just feels too long. To my mind the exploration log could easily be dropped, it contains nothing which is not already explained in the description. The discovery description I also find redundant; perhaps put it into a separate collapsible? I know that there are people on the site who like that sort of thing, but I am not one of them. The description of the formation of the second instance could be made an addendum and also put into a collapsible. In my opinion these two minor changes would make the article less of a wall of text and thus more appealing to read.

Anyway, shamefully underrated. Deserves more attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huge thanks to Rimple and a few IRL people who looked at this, hope y'all like it.
